deep.en: going far down from the top or surface

ex.en: The lake is very deep near the middle.
ex.ko: 그 호수는 가운데 부분이 매우 깊다.

deep.en: showing serious and careful thought or strong feeling

ex.en: They had a deep discussion about their future.
ex.ko: 그들은 미래에 대해 깊은 대화를 나눴다.

ex.en: Breathe deep and try to relax.
ex.ko: 숨을 깊이 들이쉬고 긴장을 풀어 봐.

deeply.en: to a great degree, especially in your feelings

ex.en: She was deeply moved by the film.

degree.en: the amount or level of something

ex.en: There is a high degree of risk involved.

degree.en: a qualification given by a university after finishing a course of study

ex.en: She has a degree in economics.
The temperature dropped to five degrees.

delay.en: make something happen later than planned

ex.en: Heavy snow delayed the flight for two hours.

delay.en: a period of time when something is made to wait

ex.en: There was a long delay at the airport.

dentist.en: a person whose job is to treat people's teeth

ex.en: I have an appointment with the dentist tomorrow.

department store.cue: 여러 매장이 한 건물에 모인 큰 상점
department store.en: a large store that sells many different kinds of goods in separate sections

ex.en: She bought a new coat at the department store.

depend.en: be decided or affected by something

ex.en: Our plans depend on the weather.

depend.en: rely on someone or something for help or support

ex.en: Children depend on their parents for food and shelter.
ex.ko: 아이들은 먹을 것과 잠잘 곳을 부모에게 의존한다.

description.en: a piece of writing or speech that says what someone or something is like

ex.en: Give me a description of the missing bag.

desert.en: leave a person or place, especially when you should stay

ex.en: He deserted his family when they needed him most.
ex.ko: 그는 가족이 가장 필요로 할 때 그들을 저버렸다.

desert.en: a large area of land with very little rain and few plants

ex.en: Very few people live in the desert.

dessert.en: a sweet food eaten at the end of a meal

ex.en: What would you like for dessert?

destroy.en: damage something so badly that it no longer exists or works

ex.en: The fire destroyed the whole building.

detail.en: a small piece of information about something

ex.en: Please give me all the details of the trip.
ex.ko: 여행에 대한 모든 세부사항을 알려 주세요.

develop.en: create or improve something over time

ex.en: The company developed a new phone app.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새로운 휴대폰 앱을 개발했다.

develop.en: grow or change gradually into a more advanced state

ex.en: The town has developed a lot in the last ten years.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 지난 10년 동안 많이 발전했다.

develop.en: begin to have a problem or illness, gradually

ex.en: She developed a bad cough last winter.

developed.en: (of a country) having a strong economy and industry

ex.en: Japan is a highly developed country.

diamond.en: a hard, clear precious stone used in jewelry

ex.en: He gave her a diamond ring.

diary.en: a book in which you write down what happens to you each day

ex.en: She writes in her diary every night.
Her grandfather died last year.

die.en: (of a machine or engine) stop working suddenly

ex.en: The car engine died in the middle of the road.

diet.en: the food that a person or animal usually eats

ex.en: A healthy diet includes plenty of fruit and vegetables.
ex.ko: 건강한 식단에는 과일과 채소가 많이 포함된다.

diet.en: a plan to eat less or eat certain foods, often to lose weight

ex.en: He went on a diet to lose five kilograms.
People see the same problem differently.

difficulty.en: a problem or something that makes things hard to do

ex.en: She had difficulty finding a job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 직장을 구하는 데 어려움을 겪었다.

digital camera.en: a camera that stores pictures electronically instead of on film

ex.en: I bought a new digital camera for the trip.
ex.ko: 나는 여행을 위해 새 디지털 카메라를 샀다.

dinosaur.en: a large reptile that lived millions of years ago

ex.en: Kids love learning about dinosaurs.
ex.ko: 아이들은 공룡에 대해 배우는 것을 좋아한다.

direct.en: going straight from one place to another without stopping

ex.en: We took a direct flight to London.

direct.en: tell people what to do or how to do something

ex.en: The officer directed traffic at the busy intersection.
ex.ko: 그 경찰관은 혼잡한 교차로에서 교통을 지휘했다.

direct.en: control how a film or play is made or performed

ex.en: She directed her first film at the age of twenty-five.
ex.ko: 그녀는 스물다섯 살에 첫 영화를 감독했다.

direction.en: the way something moves or points toward

ex.en: We were walking in the wrong direction.

direction.en: an instruction that tells you how to do something or how to get somewhere

ex.en: Follow the directions on the medicine bottle.

director.en: the person who controls how a film or play is made

ex.en: The director praised the actors after the show.
ex.ko: 그 감독은 공연 후 배우들을 칭찬했다.

director.en: a person who manages a company or organization

ex.en: She was promoted to director of marketing.

disadvantage.en: something that makes a situation more difficult or less good

ex.en: Living far from the city has some disadvantages.
ex.ko: 도시에서 멀리 사는 것은 몇 가지 단점이 있다.

disagree.en: have a different opinion from someone else

ex.en: I disagree with your opinion on this matter.
ex.ko: 나는 이 문제에 대한 네 의견에 동의하지 않아.

disappear.en: stop being visible or seem to no longer exist

ex.en: The cat disappeared into the bushes.

disappointed.en: unhappy because something was not as good as you hoped

ex.en: I was disappointed with my test score.
The movie's ending was disappointing.

disco.en: a place or type of music for dancing to popular songs

ex.en: We danced all night at the disco.

discover.en: find something for the first time, or find out information

ex.en: Scientists discovered a new species of fish.

discussion.en: a conversation in which people talk about a topic and share ideas

ex.en: We had a long discussion about the project.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 프로젝트에 대해 긴 토의를 했다.

dishonest.en: not honest; likely to lie or cheat

ex.en: It was dishonest of him to hide the truth.
ex.ko: 진실을 숨긴 것은 그의 부정직한 행동이었다.

ex.en: She dislikes loud noises.

dislike.en: a feeling of not liking someone or something

ex.en: He has a strong dislike for spicy food.
ex.ko: 그는 매운 음식에 강한 반감을 가지고 있다.

display.en: show something so people can see it

ex.en: The shop displayed its new products in the window.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 새 상품을 진열창에 전시했다.

display.en: a collection of things shown to the public

ex.en: There is a fireworks display tonight.

display.en: the screen of an electronic device that shows information

ex.en: The phone has a large, bright display.
ex.ko: 그 휴대폰은 크고 밝은 화면을 가지고 있다.

disturb.en: interrupt someone while they are doing something, or upset the peace of a place

ex.en: Please don't disturb your sister, she's studying.
Divide the cake into eight pieces.

divide.en: a serious disagreement or difference between two groups

ex.en: There is a growing divide between rich and poor.
ex.ko: 부유층과 빈곤층 사이의 격차가 커지고 있다.

dizzy.en: feeling like everything is spinning and you might fall

ex.en: I felt dizzy after standing up too fast.
I'm done with my homework.

done.en: (of food) fully cooked and ready to eat

ex.en: The steak isn't done yet.
ex.ko: 그 스테이크는 아직 다 익지 않았다.

double.ko: 두 배로 하다, 두 배가 되다
double.en: become or make something twice as large or as many

ex.en: The company doubled its profits last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 수익을 두 배로 늘렸다.

double.en: twice as much or as many

ex.en: He asked for a double portion of rice.
ex.ko: 그는 밥 두 배 분량을 달라고 했다.

doubt.en: feel unsure that something is true

ex.en: I doubt that he will come to the party.

doubt.en: a feeling of being unsure about something

ex.en: There is no doubt that she worked hard.
ex.ko: 그녀가 열심히 일했다는 것은 의심의 여지가 없다.

download.en: copy a file or program from the internet onto your device

ex.en: I downloaded the app onto my phone.

downstairs.en: on or to a lower floor of a building

ex.en: She went downstairs to answer the door.

downstairs.en: on a lower floor of a building

ex.en: The downstairs bathroom needs cleaning.

downtown.en: in or to the central or main business area of a city

ex.en: We went downtown to do some shopping.

downtown.en: the central or main business area of a city

ex.en: Her office is in downtown Seoul.

drawer.en: a sliding box built into a piece of furniture, used for storing things

ex.en: He put the papers in the top drawer.
ex.ko: 그는 서류를 맨 위 서랍에 넣었다.

drawing.en: a picture made with a pencil or pen, not paint

ex.en: The child made a drawing of her family.
ex.ko: 그 아이는 자기 가족을 그림으로 그렸다.

drawing.en: an event in which winners are chosen randomly, often for a prize

ex.en: She won a car in the prize drawing.
She got dressed quickly and left the house.
ex.ko: 그녀는 재빨리 옷을 입고 집을 나섰다.

drill.en: a tool used for making holes

ex.en: He used a drill to hang the picture.

drill.en: make a hole in something using a drill

ex.en: The workers drilled through the wall.

drill.en: repeated practice used to teach a skill

ex.en: The soldiers did a fire drill every month.
ex.ko: 군인들은 매달 화재 대피 훈련을 했다.

driver's license.cue: 차를 운전할 수 있음을 증명하는 카드
driver's license.en: an official document that allows you to drive a car

ex.en: He finally got his driver's license last week.

drug.en: a substance used as medicine to treat illness

ex.en: This drug can reduce fever quickly.

drug.en: an illegal substance that affects the mind and can be addictive

ex.en: The police arrested him for selling drugs.
ex.ko: 경찰은 마약 판매 혐의로 그를 체포했다.

drugstore.en: a shop that sells medicine and other everyday items

ex.en: I need to stop by the drugstore for some medicine.
ex.ko: 약을 좀 사러 약국에 들러야 한다.

duck.en: a bird that swims on water and has a wide flat beak

ex.en: The ducks swam across the pond.

duck.en: lower your head or body quickly to avoid being hit

ex.en: He ducked to avoid the low branch.
ex.ko: 그는 낮은 나뭇가지를 피하려고 몸을 숙였다.

during.en: all through a particular period of time, or at some point within it

ex.en: She fell asleep during the movie.

dust.cue: 공기 중에 떠다니거나 물건에 쌓이는 가루
dust.en: fine, dry powder that collects on surfaces

ex.en: There was a thick layer of dust on the shelf.
ex.ko: 선반 위에 두꺼운 먼지가 쌓여 있었다.

dust.en: remove dust from a surface by wiping it

ex.en: She dusted the furniture before the guests arrived.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손님이 오기 전에 가구의 먼지를 닦았다.

ex.en: He earns a good salary as a teacher.

earn.en: gain something because of your efforts or good behavior

ex.en: She earned the respect of her colleagues.

earring.en: a piece of jewelry worn on the ear

ex.en: She wore small silver earrings.

earth.en: the planet on which we live

ex.en: The Earth orbits around the sun.
The seeds were buried in the earth.

earthquake.en: a sudden shaking of the ground caused by movement underground

ex.en: A strong earthquake hit the city last night.
ex.ko: 어젯밤 그 도시에 강한 지진이 발생했다.

ex.en: She easily solved the puzzle.

east.en: the direction where the sun rises

ex.en: The sun rises in the east.
They drove east for two hours.

editor.en: a person who checks and corrects writing before it is published

ex.en: The editor made several changes to the article.
ex.ko: 편집자는 그 기사에 여러 수정을 했다.

education.en: the process of teaching and learning, especially in schools

ex.en: Every child has the right to education.

educational.en: related to education, or helping people learn

ex.en: This is an educational program for children.

effect.en: a change that happens because of something else

ex.en: The medicine had a strong effect on her headache.
ex.ko: 그 약은 그녀의 두통에 강한 효과가 있었다.

effort.en: physical or mental energy used to try to do something

ex.en: She put a lot of effort into her studies.

elderly.cue: 나이가 많은 사람을 정중히 부를 때
elderly.en: old, especially used as a polite way to describe older people

ex.en: We help elderly neighbors carry groceries.
ex.ko: 우리는 나이 든 이웃들이 장을 나르는 것을 도와준다.

ex.en: She bought an electric kettle.

elephant.en: a very large gray animal with a long trunk

ex.en: Elephants are the largest land animals.

elevator.en: a machine that carries people up and down between floors of a building

ex.en: Let's take the elevator to the tenth floor.

elsewhere.en: in, at, or to another place

ex.en: If this shop doesn't have it, we can look elsewhere.
ex.ko: 이 가게에 없으면 다른 곳에서 찾아보면 된다.

embarrassing.en: making you feel shy, ashamed, or uncomfortable

ex.en: It was embarrassing to trip in front of everyone.
ex.ko: 모든 사람 앞에서 넘어진 것은 창피했다.

emergency.en: a sudden, dangerous situation that needs quick action

ex.en: Call 911 in case of an emergency.
The bottle is empty.

empty.en: remove everything from inside a container

ex.en: He emptied his bag onto the table.

encourage.en: give someone support and confidence to do something

ex.en: Her parents encouraged her to follow her dream.
ex.ko: 그녀의 부모님은 그녀가 꿈을 좇도록 격려했다.

endangered.en: (of a plant or animal species) at risk of disappearing completely

ex.en: The tiger is an endangered species.

ending.en: the last part of a story, film, or event

ex.en: The movie had a happy ending.
The children were energetic all morning.

enormous.en: extremely large in size or amount

ex.en: They live in an enormous house.
We don't have enough time.
Is the soup hot enough for you?

enter.en: come or go into a place

ex.en: Please knock before you enter the room.

enter.en: register to take part in a competition or event

ex.en: She entered the singing contest last year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 작년에 그 노래 대회에 참가했다.

entertainment.en: films, music, shows, and other activities that people enjoy in their free time

ex.en: The cruise offers live music and other entertainment.
ex.ko: 그 유람선은 라이브 음악과 다른 오락거리를 제공한다.

entrance.en: the door or gate where you go into a place

ex.en: Meet me at the main entrance of the museum.

envelope.en: a paper cover used to hold and mail a letter

ex.en: She sealed the envelope and mailed the letter.

envy.en: wish you had something that someone else has

ex.en: I envy people who can speak many languages.
ex.ko: 나는 여러 언어를 할 수 있는 사람들이 부럽다.

envy.en: a feeling of wanting what someone else has

ex.en: Her success filled him with envy.
ex.ko: 그녀의 성공은 그를 부러움으로 가득 채웠다.

episode.en: one part of a TV or radio show that is broadcast separately

ex.en: I watched the latest episode of the show last night.
ex.ko: 나는 어젯밤 그 드라마의 최신 회를 봤다.

error.en: a mistake, especially one caused by carelessness

ex.en: There is a small error in this report.

escalator.en: a moving staircase that carries people between floors

ex.en: Take the escalator up to the second floor.

escape.en: get away from a dangerous or unpleasant place or situation

ex.en: The prisoner escaped from jail last night.

escape.en: an act of getting away from a place or a difficult situation

ex.en: The movie tells the story of a daring escape.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 대담한 탈출 이야기를 그린다.

especially.en: more than usual or more than others

ex.en: I love fruit, especially strawberries.
ex.ko: 나는 과일을 좋아하는데, 특히 딸기를 좋아한다.

essay.en: a short piece of writing about a particular subject

ex.en: She wrote an essay about climate change.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기후 변화에 관한 에세이를 썼다.

establish.en: start or create an organization, system, or relationship

ex.en: The company was established in 1990.

euro.en: the currency used by most countries in the European Union

ex.en: The coffee cost three euros.

even.en: used to emphasize something surprising or unexpected

ex.en: Even my little brother can solve this puzzle.
ex.ko: 내 어린 남동생조차 이 퍼즐을 풀 수 있다.

even.en: (of a number) able to be divided exactly by two

ex.en: Four and six are even numbers.
Make sure the shelf is even before you attach it.
Have you ever been to Japan?

evidence.en: facts or information that show something is true

ex.en: The police found no evidence of a crime.

exactly.en: in a precise or accurate way

ex.en: The train arrived exactly on time.

exam.en: a formal test of your knowledge or ability

ex.en: I have a math exam tomorrow.
Everyone came except Tom.

exchange.en: give something and receive something else in return

ex.en: We exchanged phone numbers before leaving.

exchange.en: an act of giving and receiving things

ex.en: They had a friendly exchange of ideas.

exchange.en: the changing of money from one currency to another

ex.en: What is today's exchange rate for dollars?

exhibition.en: a public show of art, products, or other things

ex.en: We visited a photography exhibition at the museum.
Life could exist on other planets.
ex.ko: 다른 행성에도 생명체가 존재할 수 있다.

expect.en: think that something will probably happen

ex.en: We expect a lot of rain this weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 이번 주말에 비가 많이 올 것으로 예상한다.

expect.en: believe that someone should do something

ex.en: Teachers expect students to arrive on time.

experience.en: have something happen to you, or feel something

ex.en: She experienced a lot of stress at her new job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 직장에서 많은 스트레스를 경험했다.

experience.en: knowledge or skill gained from doing or seeing things

ex.en: He has ten years of experience as a chef.

expert.cue: 어떤 분야를 아주 잘 아는 사람
expert.en: a person with special knowledge or skill in a subject

ex.en: She is an expert in marine biology.

expert.en: having special knowledge or skill in a subject

ex.en: He gave us expert advice on investing.
ex.ko: 그는 우리에게 투자에 관한 전문적인 조언을 해주었다.

explain.en: make something clear or easy to understand by describing it

ex.en: Can you explain how this machine works?
ex.ko: 이 기계가 어떻게 작동하는지 설명해 줄 수 있어?

explanation.en: a statement that makes something clear or easy to understand

ex.en: He gave a simple explanation of the rules.
ex.ko: 그는 규칙에 대해 간단한 설명을 해주었다.

explore.en: travel around a place to learn about it

ex.en: They explored the old city on foot.
ex.ko: 그들은 걸어서 그 옛 도시를 탐험했다.

explore.en: examine or think about an idea carefully

ex.en: The report explores the causes of climate change.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 기후 변화의 원인을 탐구한다.

express.en: show your thoughts or feelings through words or actions

ex.en: She expressed her thanks to the whole team.

express.cue: 정거장을 거의 서지 않는 빠른 교통편
express.en: traveling quickly and stopping at few or no stations

ex.en: We took the express train to save time.

expression.en: the look on someone's face that shows their feelings

ex.en: He had a worried expression on his face.

expression.en: a word or phrase used in a particular way

ex.en: "Break a leg" is an expression that means good luck.

extra.en: more than what is usual or expected

ex.en: Could I have an extra blanket, please?
ex.ko: 여분의 담요를 하나 더 주실 수 있나요?

extra.cue: 영화에서 대사 없이 배경으로 나오는 사람
extra.en: a minor actor with no lines, used in crowd scenes

ex.en: He worked as an extra in a war movie.
The exam was extremely difficult.

fact.en: a piece of information that is true

ex.en: It's a fact that the earth is round.
The plan failed because of bad weather.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 나쁜 날씨 때문에 실패했다.

fail.en: not pass a test or course

ex.en: He failed his driving test twice.
ex.ko: 그는 운전면허 시험에 두 번 떨어졌다.

ex.en: The test was fairly easy.

fairly.en: in a way that treats everyone equally and honestly

ex.en: The teacher treats all students fairly.
ex.ko: 그 선생님은 모든 학생을 공정하게 대한다.

familiar.en: well known to you, or easy to recognize

ex.en: This song sounds familiar to me.
They ate dinner at a fancy restaurant.

fancy.en: like or want something (informal, mainly British)

ex.en: Do you fancy going to the movies tonight?
ex.ko: 오늘 밤 영화 보러 가고 싶어?

ex.en: We had a fantastic time at the beach.
The station is not far from here.
We walked far into the forest.

fare.en: the money you pay to travel on a bus, train, or taxi

ex.en: The bus fare went up this year.
The documentary was fascinating from start to finish.

fashion.en: the popular style of clothes or behavior at a certain time

ex.en: She works in the fashion industry.

fast food.en: food that is prepared and served very quickly

ex.en: They eat fast food too often.

fault.en: the responsibility for something bad that happened

ex.en: It's not your fault that the train was late.
ex.ko: 기차가 늦은 건 네 잘못이 아니야.

fault.en: a problem or weakness in something

ex.en: The mechanic found a fault in the engine.

favor.en: something you ask someone to do for you as a kind act

ex.en: Can I ask you a favor?

favor.en: like or support one thing more than another

ex.en: Most voters favor the new policy.
Many people fear spiders.

fear.en: the feeling you get when something is dangerous or threatening

ex.en: She spoke with fear in her voice.

feather.en: one of the light, soft things that cover a bird's body

ex.en: A bright feather fell from the bird's wing.
ex.ko: 밝은색 깃털 하나가 새의 날개에서 떨어졌다.

feature.cue: 어떤 것을 다른 것과 구별 짓는 점
feature.en: an important or noticeable part or quality of something

ex.en: This phone has many useful features.
ex.ko: 이 휴대폰에는 유용한 기능이 많이 있다.

feature.en: include something as an important part

ex.en: The concert features several famous singers.
ex.ko: 그 콘서트는 여러 유명 가수를 특별 출연시킨다.

fee.en: an amount of money you pay for a service

ex.en: The school charges an annual fee for tuition.

female.en: belonging to the sex that can have babies or lay eggs

ex.en: The company hired three female engineers.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 여성 엔지니어 세 명을 채용했다.

female.en: a person or animal that can have babies or lay eggs

ex.en: The zoo welcomed a new female panda.

fence.en: a barrier made of wood or wire that surrounds an area

ex.en: They built a fence around the garden.

few.en: not many; a small number of

ex.en: Very few people came to the meeting.

fiction.en: stories about imaginary people and events

ex.en: She prefers reading fiction to non-fiction.
ex.ko: 그녀는 논픽션보다 소설을 읽는 것을 더 좋아한다.

fighter.en: a fast military plane used for attacking enemy aircraft

ex.en: The fighter jets flew over the city.

fighter.cue: 직접 싸우는 사람, 권투 선수 등
fighter.en: a person who fights, especially as a boxer or soldier

ex.en: He trained hard to become a professional fighter.
ex.ko: 그는 프로 파이터가 되기 위해 열심히 훈련했다.

figure.en: a number that represents an amount

ex.en: Sales figures rose last quarter.

figure.en: a person, especially a well-known one

ex.en: She is an important figure in modern art.

figure.en: calculate a number, or work something out by thinking

ex.en: I can't figure out the answer to this question.
ex.ko: 이 문제의 답을 도무지 알아내지 못하겠다.

film.en: a story shown as moving pictures with sound

ex.en: We watched a great film last night.
The scene was filmed in Paris.
This is the final chapter of the book.

final.en: the last match in a competition, which decides the winner

ex.en: Our team made it to the final.

finally.en: after a long time or delay

ex.en: After hours of waiting, the bus finally arrived.
ex.ko: 몇 시간을 기다린 끝에 버스가 마침내 도착했다.

first name.en: the name that comes before your family name

ex.en: Please write your first name here.

fisherman.en: a person who catches fish for a living or for fun

ex.en: The fisherman went out to sea early in the morning.
ex.ko: 그 어부는 이른 아침에 바다로 나갔다.

fit.en: suitable or right for a purpose

ex.en: This job isn't fit for beginners.
She exercises every day to stay fit.

fit.en: be the right size or shape for someone or something

ex.en: These shoes fit me perfectly.
He fixed the leaking pipe himself.

flea.cue: 동물의 몸에 붙어 피를 빠는 작은 벌레
flea.en: a very small jumping insect that bites and lives on animals

ex.en: The dog was scratching because of fleas.
ex.ko: 그 개는 벼룩 때문에 긁고 있었다.

ex.en: Our flight to Tokyo leaves at nine.

float.en: stay on the surface of a liquid or in the air without sinking

ex.en: The leaves floated on the surface of the pond.

flood.en: cover an area with water that has overflowed

ex.en: Heavy rain flooded the streets downtown.

flood.en: a large amount of water covering an area that is usually dry

ex.en: The flood destroyed many homes.

flour.en: a fine powder made from grain, used to make bread and cakes

ex.en: She mixed flour, eggs, and milk to make batter.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밀가루, 달걀, 우유를 섞어 반죽을 만들었다.

fog.cue: 공기 중 작은 물방울로 시야가 흐려지는
fog.en: thick cloud close to the ground that makes it hard to see

ex.en: Thick fog delayed all the flights this morning.
ex.ko: 짙은 안개로 오늘 아침 모든 항공편이 지연되었다.

folder.en: a cover used to hold loose papers together, or a place on a computer to store files

ex.en: Save the document in the new folder.

follow.en: go or come after someone or something

ex.en: The dog followed her all the way home.
ex.ko: 그 개는 집까지 계속 그녀를 따라갔다.

follow.en: act according to a rule, instruction, or piece of advice

ex.en: Please follow the safety instructions.
